stages.title: A blank canvas — 34 048 × 5 312 pixels
stages.notes: Trivial: white 34048x5312 frame at the canonical downsample.

stages.title: The official Paris 1:5000 plan
stages.notes: The original pre-rotation map is NOT in the repo (upstream input, author Mathis Koroglu). Obtain it before this stage can be rendered. Do not fabricate.
stages.chronology_note: History: the upstream map work predates this repo (canny extraction authored 2025-11-19, per docs/DATA_PROVENANCE.md).

stages.title: Positioning — the Grand Axis turned horizontal (−20.3°)
stages.notes: Derivable once s01's source exists: animate the 20.3° cw rotation and band placement. Angle and georeference are known (decomposition/meta/georeference.json).
stages.chronology_note: History: rotation was applied upstream, before canny_final.tiff existed (pre-repo, 2025).

stages.title: Extraction — the street network as pure line-work
stages.notes: End state exists in repo. Transition from s02 if source obtained, else from s00.
stages.chronology_note: History: canny_final.tiff authored 2025-11-19 (upstream); entered the repo as stage-0 input at the initial import (2026-07-08).

stages.title: Cleanup — 24 artifacts removed, the canonical master
stages.notes: Exactly replayable: 8743 documented pixels across 24 components (removal_manifest.csv). Animate each removal with brief zoom-insets; qa before/after crops as reference.

stages.title: The regions arrive — two flanking panels
stages.notes: Starting partitions are panel-local: composite at global offsets (left +0, right +28048) before downsampling.
stages.chronology_note: History: the extremity partition PREDATES the diem repo — created in the fresco_building project (MIT, 2025); imported 2026-07-08. Narrative places it after the center master.

stages.title: Optimization — every region gets its true proportional area
stages.generator: cd extremities && python optimize.py both  (re-emits frames if needed)
stages.notes: REUSE: convergence GIFs exist (2808/2987 recorded steps) but are panel-local — re-render both panels side by side on the full canvas for canonical previews. 0.00% final area error is the payoff beat.
stages.chronology_note: History: the optimization ran in the fresco_building project (pre-repo, 2025); outputs integrated 2026-07-09 (dd74512). Chronologically BEFORE stage-2 decomposition — narrative order is dramaturgical.

stages.title: Decomposition — banks, arrondissements, quartiers, superblocks, cells
stages.generator: cd pipeline/stage2 && python s07_viz.py  (regenerates the level maps)
stages.notes: Level maps are already full-canvas. Sequence L1->L2->L3->L4->L5 as overlay pulses on the master. Narrative position after s06 reads better — confirm at full-cut review.
stages.chronology_note: History: stage 2, 2026-07-08 (initial import 0646dac) — chronologically before the extremities were integrated into this repo.

stages.title: Generation — each superblock and region receives its image
stages.notes: Stage 3/4. Capture rule: every accepted tile (starting with pilots L5_sb034, L5_seine_corridor strip, EXT_L_bretagne) saves a full-canvas snapshot at acceptance time — this stage's frames accumulate as the work happens.
stages.chronology_note: Future — chronology will match narrative from here on (capture rule).

stages.title: Compositing — the fresco assembles
stages.notes: Layer order per production/specs/fresco.yaml#compositing_order (draft). Snapshot after each layer lands: fills, corridor, islands, bridges, extremities, ink recomposite.

stages.title: Laurel and oak — the seams become the Paris coat of arms
stages.notes: Blocked on branch design (generation/prompt_tables/seam_ornaments.json is blocked_on_design). Laurel left, then oak right; git_ref marks the strategy decision, not artwork.
stages.chronology_note: Future — the strategy decision is 2026-07-09 (2855a4e); artwork does not exist.

stages.title: Diem — the finished fresco
stages.notes: Final hold on the validated production master (production/masters/, once it exists).

order_semantics: 'order' is the NARRATIVE playback order (dramaturgical), not project chronology; per-stage 'chronology_note' preserves history
